Functions for managing Bluetooth device identity, including setting device names and addresses.

# Purpose
This C header file provides function declarations for managing Bluetooth driver identity and address configurations. It includes functions to set the local device name with [`bt_driver_id_set_local_device_name`](<#bt_driver_id_set_local_device_name>), copy the local identity address using [`bt_driver_id_copy_local_identity_address`](<#bt_driver_id_copy_local_identity_address>), and configure the local address for on-air use with [`bt_driver_set_local_address`](<#bt_driver_set_local_address>). The file also includes a function to copy a unique chip information string for manufacturing purposes, [`bt_driver_id_copy_chip_info_string`](<#bt_driver_id_copy_chip_info_string>), and a function to generate a new private resolvable address, [`bt_driver_id_generate_private_resolvable_address`](<#bt_driver_id_generate_private_resolvable_address>). These functions are essential for configuring and managing Bluetooth device identity and address settings in a Bluetooth driver.
# Imports and Dependencies

---
- `bluetooth/bluetooth_types.h`
